The ATS needs the full URL: linkedin.com/in/[username] ŌĆö a hyperlinked label with no raw URL will be invisible to most parsers.No GitHub or portfolio link. For a SOC analyst role, linking to a GitHub with detection rules, Splunk queries, or write-ups from CyberDefenders/TryHackMe labs would significantly boost ATS keyword density and recruiter interest.Missing critical cybersecurity ATS keywords: MITRE ATT&CK framework, EDR tools (CrowdStrike, SentinelOne), SOAR, threat intelligence, Wireshark, firewall analysis, vulnerability management. These appear in nearly every SOC L1 job description.No GitHub listed and no actual job title or employer under experience ŌĆö ATS parsers look for company name + job title + dates as the core structure of an experience entry. If the exam has been passed, move it to a dedicated "Certifications" section with the format: "CompTIA Security+ (SY0-701) ŌĆö CompTIA, Jan 2024." Add completion context: "Completed IBM QRadar SOC Analyst Track ŌĆö ranked in top X% of learners" if that data is available from CyberDefenders."SOC Investigation ŌĆö YouTube (Jan 2025)" weakens the training section. The entire experience section is self-directed lab work on CyberDefenders and TryHackMe. Standardize to "YouTube (Jan 2025)" format throughout.The professional summary opens with the job title in bold ("SOC Analyst L1") ŌĆö this is a strong ATS and recruiter signal and is done correctly.

ATS systems specifically look for a "Work Experience" or "Professional Experience" section anchored with employer + title + dates. Without one, the CV may score very low on experience parsing regardless of actual content quality.Missing critical .NET ATS keywords: Docker, Azure, unit testing (xUnit/NUnit), Swagger/OpenAPI, Postman, Dapper, CI/CD, Entity Framework migrations. cv_text: Ahmed Ibrahim El-Kassas
notes:
ATS Compatibility Ś 0/2 | Poor
?? The CV is built using a two-column layout with an embedded QR code image. When an ATS reads this PDF, it scrambles the section order Ś for example, Education and Certifications appear before the Professional Summary, and Personal Information appears between Projects and Languages. This means the ATS cannot correctly identify or categorize any section. Fix: rebuild the CV as a single-column plain-text document with no images or columns.
?? The QR code is an image Ś ATS systems cannot read images. It needs to be removed completely and replaced with the plain URL written out in text (e.g., github.com/ahmed-elkassas).
?? Dates are written as "2022 to Present" and "2024 to 2025." ATS date parsers expect a standard format. Fix: change all dates to "Jan 2022 ¢ Present" and "Jan 2024 ¢ Dec 2025."
?? The Web and Application Security Tools list ends with "Nikto," followed by a line break, then continues on a new line. This can cause ATS systems to read "Nikto" and "WPScan" as one merged term. Fix: put all tools in each category on one continuous comma-separated line with no trailing commas.
?? There is no GitHub or portfolio link in the contact section. For a cybersecurity candidate who has built 15+ Python tools, this is a missed opportunity. Fix: add a GitHub profile URL directly in the header next to the LinkedIn link.
?? There is no "Core Competencies" or "Key Skills" summary block at the top. Fix: add a single line of 8¢10 top keywords (e.g., Penetration Testing, Vulnerability Assessment, Burp Suite, Metasploit, Linux Security) right below the Professional Summary so ATS systems pick them up immediately.
?? The "Personal Information" section (marital status, military service) is not a recognized ATS heading and will be ignored or misclassified. Fix: remove the section entirely. If military exemption needs to be mentioned, add it as a single word in the header line (e.g., "Military: Exempted").

Achievements Ś 2/2 | Good
? The CV includes 9 cybersecurity certifications Ś CEH, eJPT, eWPT, CBBH, CPTS, CCNA, IC3, and a Network Security Certificate. In cybersecurity, certifications are direct proof of hands-on skill and are counted as achievements. This is a strong and well-rounded set.
? CPTS (Hack The Box ¢ Certified Penetration Testing Specialist) is one of the most respected hands-on offensive security certifications available. It signals real practical ability, not just theoretical knowledge. This should be mentioned by name in the Professional Summary.
? The graduation project was presented at the Military Technical Academy ITC Conference. This is a recognized external achievement that goes beyond academics.
? "Developed more than 15 Python security tools" is a concrete, quantified achievement. It shows initiative and technical depth beyond just using existing tools.
?? MCSA and OSCP are listed with "(Course Content Studied)" next to them. This tells the reader these were not completed or passed. For OSCP especially Ś one of the most recognized names in offensive security Ś this draws attention to an incomplete attempt rather than an achievement. Fix: remove them from the Certifications section and create a separate small section called "Currently Pursuing" or "In Progress" if they are still being studied.
?? CCNA and IC3 are foundational certifications that carry less weight next to CEH, CPTS, and eWPT. Fix: group them separately under a "Foundational Certifications" sub-label so the advanced certifications get more visual prominence and are seen first.
?? The "15 Python security tools" line is buried inside a single sentence in the Experience section. Fix: give it its own bullet point and add one sentence explaining what the tools do Ś for example, "Developed 15+ custom Python tools for automated reconnaissance, vulnerability scanning, and exploit automation."
?? If any bug bounty work has produced accepted vulnerability reports, Hall of Fame listings, or CVE assignments, these must be added as separate bullet points in the Experience section. These are among the strongest achievements a cybersecurity CV can carry and are currently missing entirely.

Relevance & Structure Ś 1/2 | Needs Work
? The Professional Summary is clear and well-targeted Ś it covers the three core areas (penetration testing, Linux administration, IoT/SDN security) in three concise sentences without overpromising.

Fix: add at least one number to each section ŌĆö for example the graduation project grade, the number of RFID systems deployed at Zlious, the number of network devices configured at Wadi Degla, or any user or performance metric from the CyberWise project.ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The Zlious Tech role describes hands-on hardware experience (UHF antennas, readers, BNC connectors, Windows Server) but gives no measurable outcomes ŌĆö no number of sites installed, no number of clients served, no systems deployed. Fix: add the university graduation GPA or cumulative grade ŌĆö and if the IGCSE grade is kept, move it to a brief secondary line rather than giving it equal prominence with the university degree.Relevance & Structure ŌĆö 0/2 | PoorŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The CV has no clear professional identity ŌĆö the headline is missing entirely (the CV opens directly with the name and address), the Profile section describes a Data Science + Cybersecurity graduate, but the Experience shows Network Administration and RFID hardware work, and the Projects show a PHP web platform. These three directions are contradictory and create a fragmented professional narrative. Fix: decide on one target role ŌĆö Cybersecurity Analyst, Network Engineer, or Web Developer ŌĆö and restructure the entire CV around that single direction, removing or minimizing entries that do not support it.ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The two Cisco course completion entries are formatted as Experience entries with multi-paragraph descriptions and numbered skill lists (11 items). These are certifications, not jobs, and their placement and formatting makes the Experience section confusing and bloated. Fix: move both Cisco entries to a Certifications section with a single line each ŌĆö "Cisco Network Security ŌĆö Misr International University, Sept 2024" and "Cisco CyberOps Associate ŌĆö Misr International University, Sept 2024."ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The Projects section contains only one project ŌĆö CyberWise ŌĆö which is the graduation project. Fix: add at least one more project with a GitHub link, and expand CyberWise to include the technology stack architecture, the types of attack simulations implemented, and the project grade or outcome.ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The Experience section bullet points are written as long narrative paragraphs rather than concise professional bullets ŌĆö the Zlious Tech entry is 6 lines in a single bullet and reads like a journal entry ("In this role i have gained hands on experience on Hardware technologies as UHF Antenna..."). Fix: rewrite all experience bullets as short, single-idea sentences starting with strong action verbs ŌĆö maximum 1.5 lines per bullet, 3ŌĆō5 bullets per role.ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The Personal Skills section lists "Problem Solving, Time management, Quick Learner, Eager to learn and Communication Skills" ŌĆö these are identical to the soft skills listed in the Profile section ("quick learner with strong time management, collaboration"). Fix: remove the Personal Skills section entirely to eliminate duplication ŌĆö soft skills mentioned once in the Profile are sufficient.Writing Quality ŌĆö 0/2 | PoorŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The Profile section contains a run-on sentence: "Moreover being always passionate about technology and eager to apply my skills to contribute to innovative and impactful projects" ŌĆö "Moreover being" is grammatically incorrect and this phrase adds no professional value. Fix: remove this sentence entirely or restructure as "Passionate about applying technical skills to innovative, impact-driven projects."ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The Zlious Tech bullet uses first-person language ŌĆö "In this role i have gained," "as my role was not form office only" ŌĆö CV bullets must never use first-person pronouns ("I," "my," "i"). Fix: rewrite in third-person implied format ŌĆö "Gained hands-on experience configuring UHF hardware and Windows Server environments" ŌĆö and correct "form" to "from."ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The Wadi Degla internship opening bullet is written entirely in passive voice ŌĆö "exceptional dedication...were demonstrated," "Actively participating...were gained" ŌĆö with no subject performing the actions. Fix: rewrite in active voice with the candidate as the implicit subject ŌĆö "Demonstrated strong work ethic by actively contributing to network configuration and cybersecurity monitoring projects."ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Multiple capitalization errors appear throughout ŌĆö "i" used instead of "I" in two experience bullets, "sept" instead of "Sept" for month abbreviations, "IN this Internship" with unnecessary all-caps "IN." Fix: correct to the intended language name immediately ŌĆö "C#" or remove if already listed as C++.

Add the tools and technologies used as a line at the top of each project entry.ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ "Reference Upon Request" at the bottom is an outdated convention that adds no value. Fix: remove this line entirely ŌĆö recruiters assume references are available.Writing Quality ŌĆö 0/2 | PoorŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The Career Objective contains 9 sentences written as one unbroken paragraph ŌĆö this is not acceptable writing style for a professional CV summary. Key information is buried and impossible to skim. Fix: rewrite as 3 focused sentences or 3 bullet points ŌĆö background, specialization, and value offered.ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ "Where I have developed experience" in the Career Objective is grammatically incorrect ŌĆö "Where" should not be capitalized mid-sentence and "Where I have developed" is an awkward construction. Fix: rewrite as "in which I have developed expertise in configuring, securing, and maintaining server infrastructure."ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The IT Specialist role description repeats "Provide end-user technical support, troubleshooting PCs, Outlook, emails, printers, and network issues" twice in the same paragraph ŌĆö the exact same phrase appears twice within a few lines. Fix: remove the duplicate sentence entirely.ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ "Vim Backup" appears in the IT Specialist description ŌĆö this is likely "Veeam Backup" which is the industry-standard enterprise backup tool. Fix: correct to "Time management and task prioritization" and list "Presentation skills" as a separate item if relevant.ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ "Good Remote accesstechnologies knowledge" in the Technical Skills section is missing a space ŌĆö "access" and "technologies" are merged. Fix: correct to "Good remote access technologies knowledge" or rewrite as "Remote access tools: Remote Desktop, VPN."ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ "Handle network infrastructure (Routing &Switching)" is missing a space before "Switching" ŌĆö should be "Routing & Switching." Fix: add the space.ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The Projects section uses paragraph format for both entries ŌĆö dense technical descriptions with no bullet points, no line breaks, and no clear structure. These read as copy-pasted lab documentation rather than professional project descriptions. Fix: rewrite each as 3ŌĆō4 short bullet points, one idea per bullet, starting each with an action verb.
